metadata.title: Boeing 737 MAX — MCAS, the FAA self-certification capture, and the DPA→breach→non-prosecution escape (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the crashes, MCAS facts, the ODA self-certification, the 2024 door-plug blowout, and the 2021 DPA -> 2024 breach -> rejected plea -> 2025 NPA -> Nov-2025 dismissal are FACT (NTSB/DOJ/court records, news of record); the 'accountability failure / regulatory capture' vs 'remediated + still-safe-aviation' readings are presented BOTH ways with dates
metadata.frame: The corporate-safety + regulatory-capture case, and a prosecutorial-deferral variant of liability containment. Boeing's 737 MAX used MCAS (a single-AoA-sensor flight-control system) hidden from pilots; two crashes killed 346 (Lion Air 610, 2018; Ethiopian 302, 2019), grounding the fleet 2019-2020. The FAA had delegated much certification to Boeing itself (ODA) — the capture. Boeing then escaped a criminal CONVICTION through a 2021 deferred-prosecution agreement, which it BREACHED (2024 Alaska door-plug blowout), a rejected guilty plea (Dec 2024), and finally a 2025 NON-prosecution agreement; the judge dismissed the case (Nov 2025) while saying it 'fails to secure the necessary accountability.' Attaches to Liability_Engineering, FAA, DOJ.

the_crashes: The 737 MAX added MCAS (Maneuvering Characteristics Augmentation System) to compensate for larger engines' handling; it could trigger off a SINGLE angle-of-attack sensor and repeatedly push the nose down, and it was not disclosed in pilot manuals/training. Lion Air Flight 610 (29 Oct 2018, 189 dead) and Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 (10 Mar 2019, 157 dead) crashed after MCAS activations the crews could not override — 346 deaths total. Regulators worldwide grounded the MAX from Mar 2019 until late 2020. grade: fact (NTSB/foreign-investigator findings).
the_capture: The regulatory-capture core: the FAA had delegated large parts of the MAX's certification back to Boeing under its Organization Designation Authorization (ODA) — Boeing employees acting on the FAA's behalf — and key staff misled the FAA about MCAS. Post-crash reviews (DOT IG, the JATR, Congress) found the ODA self-certification + schedule/cost pressure let the MCAS risk through. Congress passed the Aircraft Certification, Safety, and Accountability Act (2020) to reform ODA. grade: fact; 'capture vs efficient delegation' is the framing dispute.
the_doorplug: The relapse: on 5 Jan 2024 a door plug blew off Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 (a 737 MAX 9) mid-flight (no deaths). NTSB found the plug left Boeing's factory missing its retaining bolts — a quality-control/process failure tied to the Spirit AeroSystems-built fuselage. Boeing agreed to re-acquire Spirit AeroSystems (2024, ~$4.7B) to re-integrate fuselage production; the FAA capped MAX output at 38/month. grade: fact.
the_criminal_escape: The liability-containment arc, dated: (1) 7 Jan 2021 — DOJ deferred-prosecution agreement (DPA) on a charge of conspiracy to defraud the FAA: $2.5B total ($243.6M penalty + $1.77B airline compensation + $500M crash-victim fund). (2) May 2024 — DOJ found Boeing BREACHED the DPA (the door-plug lapse occurred within the DPA term). (3) Jul 2024 — Boeing agreed to plead GUILTY; (4) Dec 2024 — Judge Reed O'Connor (N.D. Tex) REJECTED the plea as 'not in the public interest' (objecting to a DEI-tinged monitor-selection clause; families also called it too lenient). (5) 23 May 2025 — DOJ + Boeing reached a NON-prosecution agreement (~$1.1B + compliance program) that let Boeing AVOID a guilty plea/felony conviction. (6) 6 Nov 2025 — O'Connor DISMISSED the case at DOJ's request, writing that the families 'are correct' the deal 'fails to secure the necessary accountability,' but that he lacked authority to deny dismissal; families are appealing. grade: fact (DOJ/court record).
the_two_sides: Both ways, dated: (A) ACCOUNTABILITY-FAILURE reading — 346 people died from a concealed system enabled by a captured certification process, and Boeing ultimately escaped a criminal conviction via DPA->NPA despite breaching its own agreement; corporate impunity. (B) BOEING/REMEDIATION reading — Boeing paid ~$2.5B then ~$1.1B, fixed MCAS, accepted a monitor + safety investment, re-acquired Spirit to fix QC, replaced leadership (Kelly Ortberg, 2024), and aviation remains statistically very safe; the door-plug was a process lapse, not a design defect. grade: both documented; the criminal-accountability outcome is the contested core.
the_honest_reading: The 737 MAX is a documented safety + regulatory-capture failure (MCAS hidden, ODA self-certification, 346 dead) followed by a documented escape from criminal CONVICTION: a 2021 DPA, a 2024 breach, a rejected 2024 plea, a 2025 non-prosecution agreement, and a Nov-2025 dismissal in which the judge himself said accountability was not secured. For the Liability_Engineering thread it is the PROSECUTORIAL-DEFERRAL variant — neither bankruptcy two-step nor preemption, but DPA/NPA used to convert a felony exposure into fines. The remediation (payments, monitor, Spirit re-acquisition, new leadership) is real and dated; so is the families' unresolved appeal. Both sides retained with dates; overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: DOJ — Boeing charged with 737 Max fraud conspiracy, agrees to pay over $2.5B (Jan 2021 DPA)
DOJ — United States v. The Boeing Company (case page)
CNBC — Judge dismisses Boeing criminal case at DOJ request despite skepticism (6 Nov 2025)
NPR — DOJ confirms deal to drop Boeing prosecution (NPA, May 2025)
FlightGlobal — 737 Max families urge judge to reject the non-prosecution deal (Sep 2025)
Clifford Law — families appeal the dismissal of the criminal charge

edges.raw_instrument: developed the 737 MAX; key staff deceived the FAA about MCAS (per the DOJ charge)
single-AoA-sensor MCAS, undisclosed to pilots -> Lion Air 610 (29 Oct 2018, 189) + Ethiopian 302 (10 Mar 2019, 157) = 346 dead; grounded Mar 2019-late 2020
delegated certification to Boeing via ODA self-certification (the capture); grounded then recertified the MAX; output capped at 38/month after 2024
fuselage supplier; the 5 Jan 2024 Alaska door-plug blowout (missing bolts) was a QC/process failure; Boeing re-acquired Spirit (2024, ~$4.7B)
the 346-death crashes + the 2024 door-plug breach drove the DOJ fraud case

2021 DPA $2.5B (conspiracy to defraud FAA); breach found May 2024; Jul-2024 guilty plea REJECTED by Judge O'Connor (Dec 2024); 2025 NPA ~$1.1B avoiding conviction; case DISMISSED 6 Nov 2025 (judge: 'fails to secure accountability'); families appealing
the PROSECUTORIAL-DEFERRAL variant of liability containment: DPA -> breach -> NPA converted felony exposure into fines, no criminal conviction despite 346 deaths
